summary: A rectangular compound surrounding a central multi-level building with a large red-tiled curved/gabled roof and four tall corner towers with flat platform tops. The central structure is stepped with three visible tiers of pale walls separated by white trim and small horizontal windows; the roof is symmetrical along a central ridge with stair-step brick detailing. A low white block boundary runs inside the outer brown ground area around the building.

shape.overall_form: central block with four corner towers inside a rectangular walled compound
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along the long axis (front-back)

materials.name: fence
materials.role: small decorative supports at windows (possible iron bars or fences)

elements: curved/gabled central roof with stepped brick stairs
three-tiered main block with horizontal white trim between tiers
rows of small horizontal windows on each tier
four tall corner towers (tall shafts) with flat platform tops and glowstone or light on top
low white block inner boundary/wall laid as spaced blocks
raised platforms/steps around base (stone foundation visible)
rebuild_hints: Place a rectangular dirt or terracotta ground pad, then an inner low white-spaced boundary around the central build.

Construct a central rectangular building of three stacked tiers: each tier slightly inset with white trim (quartz) bands; use sandstone (or pale block) for walls and small horizontal windows of glass.
Build a symmetrical brick stair-stepped roof along the long axis: use brick blocks and stairs to create a curved/gabled shape with a central ridge and repeated stair steps on the sides.
Add four tall square towers at each corner outside the main block: stonebrick vertical edges with sandstone or similar shaft, topped by slab-stone overhang platforms with glowstone center.
Use stone slab edging at roof eaves and around tower platforms; include small metal-looking details at window edges (fences or iron bars) if desired.
uncertainties: Exact block types for pale walls: appear to be sandstone or smooth sandstone but could be another pale block.
Small dark details near windows may be iron bars, trapdoors, or fences; material uncertain.
Ground material within the compound looks brown (dirt/terracotta) — exact block unknown.
Precise internal floor plan and openings (doors) are not visible from provided views.
Exact dimensions are estimated from visible scale; actual block counts may differ.
